title: Your Agent Setup Is a Portable Home Overlay
description: Pi and OpenCode consume one shared home layer from any project. The source repo tracks reusable behavior; secrets and volatile runtime state stay local.

coreTakeaways.title: Source of truth
coreTakeaways.summary: The agents repo is the portable source for shared skills, commands, Pi config, OpenCode config, MCP manifests, templates, and install logic.

coreTakeaways.title: Home overlay
coreTakeaways.summary: Install creates links or rendered config in ~/.agents, ~/.pi/agent, ~/.config/opencode, and ~/.config/mcpx.

coreTakeaways.title: Secrets boundary
coreTakeaways.summary: ~/.config/ai/secrets.env stays local. Pi and OpenCode inject values and block obvious reads, listings, and shell leaks.

coreTakeaways.title: Project binding
coreTakeaways.summary: Global setup supplies behavior. The current project supplies source files, git state, AGENTS.md, docs, and artifacts.

wrapperStatus.notes: backend, HTTPS, reports, route, and launchd all OK

wrapperFlow.detail: ./install.sh previews; ./install.sh --apply copies env only if missing and renders LaunchAgent plist
service.sh start loads env, resolves backend host, and lets launchd own launchd-run.sh
Only bare opencode is wrapped; args call real CLI; attach uses --dir "$PWD"
REPORTS_SERVE=1 starts reports-server.py; /reports/ can route to the sidecar

boundaryRules.detail: Rules, prompts, templates, manifests, and configs are tracked; caches, DBs, sessions, OAuth, cookies, and logs are not.
Installer previews by default; writes require --apply and back up changed non-secret config.
Secret examples may be copied if missing, but real secret targets are skipped and chmod 600.
render-opencode-mcp validates JSON, local commands, and required secret names without printing values.

riskLedger.mitigation: Local secrets.env, chmod 600, runtime injectors, read/list/shell blocks
riskLedger.residual: Shells and third-party tools still need disciplined usage
Server installs can drift outside repo

riskLedger.mitigation: service.sh doctor probes launchd, HTTP, HTTPS, reports
riskLedger.residual: Tailscale and port listeners remain environmental
Pick one convention later if shared maps matter

riskLedger.mitigation: context-audit and disabled expensive tool families

props.title: Runtime setup
props.description: Pi, OpenCode, wrapper, and project behavior as separate scan paths. Tabs keep contexts adjacent without making one giant table.

props.caption: Pi is the primary coding harness: global packages, project context, extensions, and high-trust tooling.
Standalone CLI plus rendered config, shared skills, custom agents, plugins, MCP, and local keybinds.
Separate optional repo. It starts opencode web under launchd and exposes reports when enabled.
Global behavior plus local project facts

props.title: Source ownership and boundaries
props.description: The contract is tight: track reusable behavior, never track volatile runtime state or secrets.

props.caption: What the source repo owns and what it refuses to own.
Operational guardrails from the source report.

props.title: Risks, checks, and failure modes
props.description: The setup is conservative, but not magic. These are the residual edges.

props.caption: Safe validation commands recorded by the source report

props.text: Canonical local file: ~/.config/ai/secrets.env. Pi and OpenCode inject values into runtime env, block secret-path reads/listing/shell references, and Pi also blocks obvious env dumps.
Installers copy example files only if targets are missing, then chmod 600. Real OAuth, cookies, sessions, caches, DBs, and secrets are never migrated.
The repo tracks manifests, not server installs. node scripts/render-opencode-mcp.mjs --check validates JSON and prints required secret names or missing local command paths, never secret values.
launchd, Tailscale, report routes, and local port listeners can drift independently. ./service.sh doctor prints status, HTTP probes, launchd state, report health, logs, and next action.
